plan.topic: Issue #59 codex nx MCP launcher portability fix

issues.title: Decide the correct upstream codex harness behavior for nx MCP configuration in generated subagent specs
issues.decision: Selected: generated Codex subagent specs should preserve per-role nx disabled_tools without hardcoding a bare nx MCP launcher, allowing the parent session’s already-valid MCP launcher configuration to remain authoritative. Rationale: official Codex docs describe custom agent files as configuration layers and say optional fields such as mcp_servers inherit from the parent session when omitted; current nexus-core output is stronger than necessary because it injects command = "nexus-mcp" whenever disabled_tools are present. Rejected alternative 1: keep the hardcoded bare command and treat this as downstream-only — rejected because the portability assumption originates in nexus-core generation. Rejected alternative 2: redesign the whole Codex restriction model away from mcp_servers.nx — rejected because issue #59 can be solved safely without broad architectural churn.

issues.title: Apply the minimal safe fix for issue #59 with regression coverage, keeping it independent from #57 and #58 changes
issues.decision: Selected: make the Codex nx MCP launcher config optional in source types and renderer output, remove the hardcoded launcher from harness/codex/agent-rules.yml, and extend generation tests so Codex agents still emit [mcp_servers.nx] with disabled_tools but no longer force command = "nexus-mcp". Rationale: this is the smallest upstream change that fixes the portability bug while preserving current per-role tool restrictions. Rejected alternative 1: add full command+args support in the same change — rejected for now because issue #59 does not require explicit child-launcher emission once inheritance is preserved. Rejected alternative 2: patch generated artifacts only — rejected because the source renderer and harness rules would simply reintroduce the problem.

plan.research_summary: Verified GitHub issue #59 (opened Apr 22, 2026). Cross-checked local source: harness/codex/agent-rules.yml hardcodes nx_mcp_server.command: nexus-mcp, and src/generate/renderers/codex.ts always emits command = CODEX_AGENT_RULES.nx_mcp_server.command inside [mcp_servers.nx] whenever disabled_tools are needed. Existing codex generation tests assert the stanza exists but do not guard against the PATH-dependent launcher assumption. Cross-checked official Codex docs today: custom agent files are configuration layers for spawned sessions; optional fields such as mcp_servers inherit from the parent session when omitted (https://developers.openai.com/codex/subagents, lines 617-625 observed today). Codex config also supports mcp_servers.<id>.command, args, and disabled_tools as independent keys (https://developers.openai.com/codex/config-reference, lines 817-834 and 825-834 observed today). Therefore the current generated child-agent stanza is unnecessarily stronger than required and can override a valid parent MCP launcher with a brittle PATH-only command.

tasks.title: Patch Codex harness rules and renderer to stop hardcoding nx MCP command
tasks.context: Issue #59 is an upstream Codex generation portability bug. Codex custom agents are config layers, but nexus-core currently emits command = "nexus-mcp" inside [mcp_servers.nx] whenever disabled_tools are needed, overriding a working parent launcher with a PATH-only assumption.
tasks.acceptance:
- Codex source generation no longer requires nx_mcp_server.command to exist
- Generated Codex agent TOML still includes [mcp_servers.nx] and disabled_tools when needed
- Generated Codex agent TOML no longer forces command = "nexus-mcp" by default
tasks.approach: Make the Codex nx MCP launcher config optional, remove the hardcoded launcher from harness/codex/agent-rules.yml, and update the renderer to emit command only when explicitly configured while still emitting disabled_tools.
tasks.risk: This change relies on Codex configuration-layer inheritance for the parent MCP launcher, so the fix should stay minimal and not alter disabled_tools behavior.

tasks.title: Add regression coverage and verify Codex generation for issue #59
tasks.context: The source fix should be locked in with generation tests so the bare-command assumption does not reappear.
tasks.acceptance:
- tests/generate/sync.test.ts asserts Codex agent output does not contain command = "nexus-mcp"
- bun test tests/generate/sync.test.ts passes
- The Codex generation test still confirms disabled_tools behavior
tasks.approach: Update generation tests to assert the Codex nx MCP stanza is still present with disabled_tools but omits the hardcoded command, then run targeted tests.
tasks.risk: If assertions are too broad, harmless future launcher support changes could cause churn; keep the test focused on the current default behavior.

plan.topic: Issue #58 resume agent_id guidance ambiguity fix

issues.title: Decide the correct canonical guidance for recording and resuming subagent identifiers across planning-related specs
issues.decision: Selected: make the canonical guidance unambiguous that planning-related specs must record the concrete agent identifier returned by the spawn tool response as agent_id for later resume paths, and must not treat a human-readable assigned name as an interchangeable substitute. Rationale: official Claude Code subagent docs currently state that resumed subagents use SendMessage with the agent’s ID in the to field; the current wording in spec/skills/nx-plan/body.md and spec/agents/lead/body.md invites storing a name instead, which breaks completed-session resume semantics. Rejected alternative 1: leave nx-auto-plan wording unchanged and only patch nx-plan — rejected because the ambiguity spans more than one planning surface and would remain inconsistent. Rejected alternative 2: preserve name as an allowed fallback — rejected because the documented resume path depends on agent ID, not a presentation label.

issues.title: Apply the minimal documentation/spec fix for issue #58 with regression coverage, keeping the change independent from other issue fixes
issues.decision: Selected: apply a minimal text-only fix in the shared spec source for both English and Korean planning/lead documents, then add targeted generation tests that assert the wrong phrase is gone and the stronger agent-ID guidance is present in generated outputs. Rationale: the bug is in shared instruction text, so fixing the source specs plus regression tests gives cross-harness coverage without coupling to unrelated runtime code. Rejected alternative 1: patch only generated artifacts — rejected because generated output is derived and the ambiguity would reappear on the next sync. Rejected alternative 2: broaden the change into runtime resume implementation work — rejected because issue #58 is about incorrect guidance, not a local runtime bug in this repo.

plan.research_summary: Verified GitHub issue #58 (opened Apr 22, 2026). Cross-checked local spec source: spec/skills/nx-plan/body.md currently says to pass the spawn id 'or the name the Lead assigned' to nx_plan_analysis_add; spec/skills/nx-auto-plan/body.md is less explicit but still underspecified; spec/agents/lead/body.md also says to record either the spawn id or the assigned name. Cross-checked generated outputs: opencode render-preview reproduces the same ambiguity. Cross-checked official Claude Code subagents docs: when resuming a completed subagent, Claude uses SendMessage with the agent’s ID as the to field (https://code.claude.com/docs/en/sub-agents, lines 654-662 as observed today). Therefore the ambiguity is real and name-based storage is incompatible with documented resume semantics for completed sessions.

tasks.title: Patch planning and lead spec guidance for resume agent IDs
tasks.context: Issue #58 is a shared text/spec bug. Current guidance in planning-related specs treats spawn IDs and assigned names as interchangeable agent_id values, but official Claude resume guidance uses the agent ID for completed-session resume.
tasks.acceptance:
- English and Korean spec files no longer present the assigned name as an interchangeable agent_id
- The updated wording explicitly requires the spawn tool response agent ID for resume paths
- The change remains text-only and scoped to issue #58
tasks.approach: Edit English and Korean spec files for nx-plan, nx-auto-plan, and lead so they explicitly require the spawn tool’s returned agent ID and clarify that human-readable names are for running-session messaging only.
tasks.risk: Because this is guidance text, over-specifying harness details could become stale; keep the wording focused on the required identifier semantics.

tasks.title: Add regression coverage and verify generated outputs for issue #58
tasks.context: The source-spec fix should propagate into generated harness assets, and tests should catch future reintroduction of the ambiguous wording.
tasks.acceptance:
- tests/generate/sync.test.ts covers the removed ambiguous wording and the corrected guidance
- bun test tests/generate/sync.test.ts passes
- Generated outputs exercised by the tests reflect the corrected text
tasks.approach: Extend generation tests to assert the problematic phrase is absent and the stronger agent-ID guidance appears in generated outputs, then run targeted tests.
tasks.risk: String-based tests can be brittle if wording changes casually, so assertions should target the semantic phrases that matter.

plan.topic: Issue #57 opencode SKILL.md frontmatter evaluation and fix

issues.title: Assess whether issue #57 is technically valid and define the correct opencode-specific behavior
issues.decision: Selected: treat issue #57 as technically valid for nexus-core and fix the opencode skill renderer so emitted SKILL.md frontmatter includes a required name field equal to the skill id / directory name, and does not emit the OpenCode-unsupported triggers field. Rationale: OpenCode docs (last updated Apr 22, 2026) require name and description, allow only name/description/license/compatibility/metadata, and require name to match the skill directory regex; current src/generate/renderers/opencode.ts omits name and generated opencode skill files in dist/render-preview confirm the omission. Rejected alternative 1: close the issue as invalid — rejected because both the official docs and this repo’s generated output support the bug report. Rejected alternative 2: add name but keep triggers — rejected because it would restore loading but still leave opencode output with harness-specific unsupported frontmatter drift and unnecessary noise.

issues.title: Define and execute the minimal safe fix scope in this repo, including regression coverage and generated artifacts
issues.decision: Selected: apply the minimal safe upstream fix in this repo only where the bug originates: update the opencode renderer, add regression assertions in generation tests, then refresh generated dist artifacts and opencode render-preview output with targeted verification. Rationale: the spec source already contains name, so the defect is introduced during opencode rendering; a focused upstream fix resolves the root cause with low cross-harness risk and gives downstream packages a clean source of truth. Rejected alternative 1: edit spec/skills bodies only — rejected because renderer currently discards name for opencode, so spec-only edits would not change emitted output. Rejected alternative 2: patch downstream wrappers instead of nexus-core — rejected because the bug is upstream in nexus-core generation and should be fixed at the shared source. Rejected alternative 3: refactor claude/codex skill rendering too — rejected because the issue is opencode-specific and widening scope adds regression risk without user value.

plan.research_summary: Verified issue #57 on GitHub (opened 2026-04-22, https://github.com/moreih29/nexus-core/issues/57). Cross-checked OpenCode Agent Skills docs updated Apr 22, 2026: SKILL.md frontmatter recognizes only name, description, license, compatibility, metadata; name and description are required; name must match directory regex ^[a-z0-9]+(-[a-z0-9]+)*$. Cross-checked local code: src/generate/renderers/opencode.ts currently emits only description and preserves triggers for skills, omitting name. Cross-checked generated output: dist/render-preview/opencode/skills/{nx-plan,nx-auto-plan,nx-run}/SKILL.md lacks name and includes triggers. Cross-checked spec source: spec/skills/*/body.md includes id/name/triggers, so the drift occurs in the opencode renderer, not the spec source. No prior similar cycle found in nx_history_search.

tasks.title: Patch opencode skill renderer and generation regression tests
tasks.context: Issue #57 is valid: OpenCode requires name+description in SKILL.md frontmatter, but src/generate/renderers/opencode.ts emits only description and also carries unsupported triggers. The bug originates in opencode rendering, not in spec/skills source files.
tasks.acceptance:
- src/generate/renderers/opencode.ts emits frontmatter with name and description for skills
- opencode generated SKILL.md output no longer contains triggers
- tests/generate/sync.test.ts covers the regression for opencode skills
tasks.approach: Update the opencode skill renderer to emit name based on the skill id/directory and omit triggers for opencode skills. Extend tests/generate/sync.test.ts to assert opencode skill files contain name and do not contain triggers.
tasks.risk: If any downstream consumer was informally reading triggers from opencode SKILL.md, that metadata will disappear; current OpenCode docs show no runtime meaning for it.

tasks.title: Regenerate affected dist artifacts and verify the fix
tasks.context: After the source/test change, generated JS and tracked preview output must reflect the upstream fix so the repo remains internally consistent.
tasks.acceptance:
- bun test tests/generate/sync.test.ts passes
- bun run build succeeds
- dist/generate/renderers/opencode.js reflects the source change
- dist/render-preview/opencode/skills/{nx-plan,nx-auto-plan,nx-run}/SKILL.md include name and omit triggers
tasks.approach: Run targeted tests plus build, then refresh dist/render-preview/opencode via the sync CLI and inspect the generated SKILL.md files.
tasks.risk: Preview output is generated content; forgetting to refresh it would leave repo evidence inconsistent with the implemented fix.
